item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my first try at giving feedback to a post and I can't think of a better one to do than this one. I loved every bit of this. The concept was really interesting and original, probably because it's so hard to describe something made up of just information, but you pulled it off.

The first thing that comes to mind is that this feels more like a tale than a SCP. You could try splitting it into the bare anomaly and then a spinoff of tales involving it, but I don't know if the SCP would be half as good without the added tale aspect. Addressing the "spinoff of tales" I said before, please do that. I want to see and know so much more about these characters. I want see more of the magic they are using and know how it works.

Honestly, I am not a good enough writer to try and improve this. I do hope that a good enough writer does come by and gives you feedback to make this even better than it is now. Once again, please write some Tales with these characters. With my first forum post, you also have my first upvote. +1 all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot to love here, but hands down the best part of this for me is Joe Smith, amateur thaumaturgist. That was totally Abbadon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, Tanhony. THIS is the kind of stuff I want to write. It's so perfect, so well written, so good.

+1000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hell yeah! i generally really enjoy seeing the foundation cooperate with other groups, and this one knocks it out of the park. the library itself is so cool, and the entities made of stolen knowledge are highkey creepy. easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS WAS SO MUCH FUN TO READ. Both as a writer and a loving reader, this just did so much RIGHT. Easy +1 + I'm sharing this to friends. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept, execution, and overall feeling of the article as a whole just blew me away. Fantastic job, and an easy +1 from me.

item.title: Or, how I learned to stop worrying and love the infovore
item.content:encoded:
For a while I got really hung up on trying to understand this, but once I realized this is largely just gleeful nonsense it all fell into place. It's a pleasure to see your conceptually uninhibited ooo ooo ooo sensibilities applied to an SCP.

JACK: (quietly) The information's clearer here. Strawberry flavored.

Marry me.

But seriously, please tell me: where in god's name did you pull all of these lost bits of media from? I'll confess the only two I was already familiar with were ET II and Love's Labors Won; the rest took some googling. I could only find one goddamn result that adequately explained your reference to the lost head of The Magic Harp/Die Zauberharfe, holy fuck. I assume the Jakob Holt one isn't supposed to be anything actually extant (as his name was lost).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good stuff! Always glad to see more TWL skips being put out :D +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to have gotten the chance to crit this one, it’s amazing!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Here's me trying my hand at, well, the Serpent's Hand.

Thanks to Scottyhoff, DrMoned, Tuomey, CosmicGhostii, ShroomDispencer, DrAkimoto and sword stuff for their always valuable feedback!

Image Credits:

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Interior_6_-_abandoned_Soviet_administration,_Sukhumi.jpg (Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 4.0 International)
